data.title: Center for Children & Families offers support after shooting that killed eight children
data.incipit: In the wake of a Sunday morning shooting that killed eight children and left others injured, the Center for Children & Families is offering immediate support to those affected by the tragedy.
data.body: SHREVEPORT, La. (KSLA) - The following information was provided by the Center of Children & Families: In the wake of a Sunday morning shooting that killed eight children and left others injured, the Center for Children & Families is offering immediate support to those affected by the tragedy. The organization said the shooting has shaken the community and could have lasting emotional impacts. The Center is extending services to first responders and emergency personnel, families and loved ones of the victims, children and classmates affected by the loss, and community members experiencing grief or distress. “Our hearts are with every individual impacted by this tragedy,” Ashley June, Shreveport regional clinical director, said in a statement. “In moments like these, it is critical that our community comes together—not only to grieve, but to support one another in healing. We want people to know they are not alone, and help is available right now.” The Center said it is offering flexible options, including in-person office sessions, community-based support and telehealth services. All services will be provided at no cost to the victims’ families, the organization said. The Center will continue accepting insurance for individuals and families seeking ongoing support. Staff will be available following the Light of Hope ceremony Thursday, April 23, at The Harbor, 3004 Knight St., Building 6, in Shreveport, according to the organization. Anyone seeking support can call 318-227-8390 or visit standforhope.org/refer/. Copyright 2026 KSLA. All rights reserved.
